page: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a Bare Metal SSD server powered by an AMD EPYC 7443P processor. This x86_64 architecture system features 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs with 2 threads per core, running at a base frequency of 2.85 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, providing 5.33 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided by a 960 GB SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong computational capabilities. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is rated in the top tier (top 10%), while multi-core performance falls into the strong category (top 25%). Memory performance is highly rated, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top tier (top 10%), although raw memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks register in the average range (middle 50%). The system also shows strong performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, PassMark database operations, and single-threaded gzip compression. For lightweight AI workloads, text generation using a small 135M LLM model achieves top-tier speeds, and medium 7B model text generation performs in the top 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal server offers high resource density and dedicated hardware access without virtualization overhead, making it a viable option for workloads requiring consistent, non-shared CPU allocation. The combination of 48 vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and local SSD storage makes this server well-suited for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, web serving, and moderate language model inference tasks.
description: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a Bare Metal SSD server featuring an AMD EPYC 7443P processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 2.85 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 RAM at 3 GHz and a 960 GB local SSD. The server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores, alongside strong multi-core processing and database operation benchmarks. Lacking a GPU, it relies on its robust x86_64 architecture to handle demanding workloads. This hardware profile is highly suitable for database hosting, multi-threaded data decompression, and medium-scale language model inference. It provides dedicated bare-metal resources, ensuring consistent performance without virtualization overhead.
og_description: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a Bare Metal SSD server featuring a 24-core AMD EPYC 7443P processor, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-24c-256gb-amd is a bare metal server with 48 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and local SSD storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7443P processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs
Equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 RAM and a 960 GB local SSD
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory benchmark scores
Strong multi-core processing, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression
